_readme: Render recipe (starcomb resolution: CLI > this file > GENERIC). Tracks the generic pipeline; pin a knob here only with a measured, dataset-specific reason.

notes.spcc: narrowband mode on the composed SHO stack: R carries SII (671.6 nm), G Ha (656.28), B OIII (500.7); bandwidths at siril defaults (the corpus names the filter wheel but not the narrowband bandwidths)
notes.calib: prebuilt-master ingest: the corpus ships header-bare masters (calib/{dark,flat}_<token>.fits) — the filename token is the whole identity; per-filter darks pair with the per-filter exposures (Ha 600 s / S2 500 s / O3 400 s)
notes.capture: measured line-flux ratios S2 0.222 : Ha 1.000 : O3 0.375 (p90 0.301/1.000/0.458); O3 sky rate 1.8x Ha's (the 501 nm band sits in the skyglow); SNR parity with Ha needs ~+32 h O3 / ~+57 h S2 — Ha-rich, O3/S2-starved. Full card: results/capture_report_ngc7635_sho.md; digital palette equalization multiplies O3 noise x2.7 and S2 noise x4.5
notes.OPEN_DEFECT: the Bubble's central SPHERE — the target's defining feature, crisp in the author's finish — is missing from our renders (user screenshots in the repo root: our_photo_missing_structure.png vs reference_photo_perfect_inner_nebula_shpere*.png; the reference is vertically mirrored vs ours). MEASURED: the sphere IS in the linear data and O3 carries it most strongly (rim/surround 4.29 in O3 vs 3.00 Ha / 2.75 S2 at y1989 x1705; interior O3/Ha 0.24 vs surround 0.11 — the reference's teal sphere), but O3 runs 4-9x below Ha in absolute flux (interior 37 vs 156 e-4), so the single LINKED stretch renders the scene as the Ha image, the sphere's hue sits near grain and the chroma coring neutralizes it. Candidate ROOT CAUSE (architectural, no knob fixes this): the industry-standard SHO chain stretches each line SEPARATELY (or per-channel) before palette combination; our compose-then-one-linked-stretch cannot give a weak line its own lift. CAUTION: the linked-stretch dead-end entry was measured on calibrated BROADBAND (channel noise, no cast) — its scope does not decide the narrowband-palette regime; a per-line design must still answer the noise question that entry measured. Exonerated on this dataset: vst chroma effect ~nil, separation leak 0.000, black_point (linear-shift invariance), stars combine. The GHS finishing lift alone WORSENS the drowning (amplifies the Ha flood around the sphere). Resolve the architecture, not a workaround
